Title: Persistent Access Token — Active Token Authorizes API Request
Preconditions:
[PRE:CLEAR_SESSION]
- Platform has Authorization__EnablePersistentStorageTokenValidation=true configured
- Admin user account available
- No active persistent token revocation in effect
column9:
[HTTP] POST {{BACK_URL}}/connect/token with grant_type=password, username={{ADMIN_EMAIL}}, password={{ADMIN_PASSWORD}}, scope=offline_access
[WAIT] Receive access_token and refresh_token in response
[HTTP] GET {{BACK_URL}}/api/platform/security/currentuser with Authorization: Bearer <access_token>
[WAIT] Receive response
column10:
[STATUS] /connect/token returns HTTP 200
[BODY] access_token field present and non-empty
[STATUS] GET /api/platform/security/currentuser returns HTTP 200
[BODY] Response contains id and login fields matching the authenticated user
column11:
[API] No 4xx/5xx on either request
[CONSOLE] No server errors
column12: POST /connect/token returns non-200; access_token absent; GET /api/platform/security/currentuser returns 401 for valid active token

Title: Persistent Access Token — Revoked Token Returns 401 (State Transition: Active → Revoked)
Preconditions:
[PRE:CLEAR_SESSION]
- Platform has Authorization__EnablePersistentStorageTokenValidation=true configured
- Admin credentials available
- Two contexts/tokens can be obtained independently
column9:
[HTTP] POST {{BACK_URL}}/connect/token credentials to obtain Token A (access_token + refresh_token)
[WAIT] Receive Token A
[HTTP] POST {{BACK_URL}}/connect/token again with same credentials to obtain Token B
[WAIT] Receive Token B (separate authorization entry)
[HTTP] POST {{BACK_URL}}/connect/revocation with token=<Token A refresh_token>, token_type_hint=refresh_token, Authorization: Bearer <Token A access_token>
[WAIT] Revocation completes (HTTP 200)
[HTTP] GET {{BACK_URL}}/api/platform/security/currentuser with Authorization: Bearer <Token A access_token>
[WAIT] Receive response (expect 401)
[HTTP] GET {{BACK_URL}}/api/platform/security/currentuser with Authorization: Bearer <Token B access_token>
[WAIT] Receive response (expect 200)
column10:
[STATUS] POST /connect/revocation returns HTTP 200
[STATUS] GET /api/platform/security/currentuser with revoked Token A returns HTTP 401 (not 200)
[BODY] 401 response does not leak user data or internal paths
[STATUS] GET /api/platform/security/currentuser with active Token B returns HTTP 200 (revocation is scoped)
column11:
[API] Token A rejected after revocation; Token B unaffected
[NETWORK] No 5xx errors on any request
column12: POST /connect/revocation returns non-200; revoked Token A still returns 200 (bug: persistent validation not active); Token B also rejected after Token A revocation (over-broad invalidation); 500 errors
column13: Obtain new token and use to clean up any test state

Title: Persistent Access Token — Never-Issued Token Returns 401
Preconditions:
[PRE:CLEAR_SESSION]
- Platform accessible
- Authorization__EnablePersistentStorageTokenValidation=true configured
column9:
[HTTP] GET {{BACK_URL}}/api/platform/security/currentuser with Authorization: Bearer eyJhbGciOiJSUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJzdWIiOiJmYWtlIn0.invalidsignature
[WAIT] Receive response (expect 401)
[HTTP] GET {{BACK_URL}}/api/platform/security/currentuser with Authorization: Bearer AAAA.BBBB.CCCC
[WAIT] Receive response (expect 401)
column10:
[STATUS] Both requests return HTTP 401
[BODY] No user data in either response
[BODY] No internal paths or stack traces in error response
column11:
[API] No 500 returned for malformed tokens
[NETWORK] Both requests fail with 401, not 200
column12: 200 returned for a fabricated/unsigned token; 500 Internal Server Error instead of 401; user data visible in error response body

Title: Session Invalidation After Password Change — Other Devices Logged Out
Preconditions:
[PRE:CLEAR_SESSION]
- Authorization__EnablePersistentStorageTokenValidation=true configured (enables OpenIddict DB-based revocation)
- User account available (use agent pool slot 1 for Device A context, slot 2 for Device B context)
- Two separate sessions can be established for the same user
column9:
[HTTP] POST {{BACK_URL}}/connect/token for @td(ACME_BUYER.email) — obtain Token-A (Device A session)
[WAIT] Receive Token-A
[HTTP] POST {{BACK_URL}}/connect/token for @td(ACME_BUYER.email) again — obtain Token-B (Device B session)
[WAIT] Receive Token-B
[HTTP] GET {{BACK_URL}}/api/platform/security/currentuser with Token-A (Device B still active — verify pre-change)
[ASSERT] Both tokens return 200 before password change
[HTTP] POST {{BACK_URL}}/api/platform/security/users/changepassword (or changePassword mutation via xAPI) using Token-A with currentPassword=@td(ACME_BUYER.password) and new password
[WAIT] Password change returns HTTP 200
[HTTP] GET {{BACK_URL}}/api/platform/security/currentuser with Token-B (Device B token — expect 401 after change)
[WAIT] Receive response
[HTTP] GET {{BACK_URL}}/api/platform/security/currentuser with Token-A (Device A token — should survive)
[WAIT] Receive response
column10:
[STATUS] Password change returns HTTP 200
[STATUS] Token-B returns HTTP 401 after password change (other-device session invalidated)
[BODY] Token-B 401 response contains no user data
[STATUS] Token-A continues to return HTTP 200 OR returns 401 (either is acceptable — behavior depends on platform implementation; the initiating-session-survives behavior is documented in VCST-4531 comment)
column11:
[API] Token-B revoked after password change
[NETWORK] No 5xx errors
[ADMIN] Security > Users panel for the account shows no active authorizations for Token-B
column12: Token-B still returns 200 after password change (invalidation not working); 500 errors on password change endpoint; Password change endpoint returns 4xx
column13: Restore original password via admin API; clean up AGENT-TEST- sessions

Title: Session Invalidation After Password Change — Storefront UX Warning Displayed
Section: Security > Session Management > Password Change > UX
Preconditions:
[PRE:CLEAR_SESSION]
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:TECHFLOW_BUYER]
- Storefront accessible; Change Password page available at /account
- Authorization__EnablePersistentStorageTokenValidation=true configured
column9:
[NAV] {{FRONT_URL}}/sign-in
[ACT] Fill 'Email': @td(TECHFLOW_BUYER.email)
[ACT] Fill 'Password': @td(TECHFLOW_BUYER.password)
[ACT] Click 'Sign In'
[WAIT] Authenticated session established
[NAV] {{FRONT_URL}}/account
[WAIT] Account page loaded
[ACT] Navigate to Change Password section
[WAIT] Change Password form visible
[ASSERT] Verify information notice or warning text is visible before submitting (as documented in VCST-4531: 'Changing your password will sign you out of all other devices and browsers.')
column10:
[DOM] Information notice visible on Change Password page containing text warning about other sessions being signed out (e.g., 'sign you out of all other devices' or equivalent wording)
[DOM] Change Password form fields visible: Current Password, New Password, Confirm Password
[STATE] User remains on the current device after seeing the notice (no auto-logout at this point — notice is pre-action)
column11:
[NETWORK] No 4xx/5xx on page load
[CONSOLE] No TypeError or JS errors
column12: Information notice absent from Change Password page (UX regression from VCST-4531 implementation); page errors with 500; console TypeError
column13: Restore password if changed via admin; [PRE:SIGNOUT]
